goal: Tell him honestly the minimum USD subset needed for a Tuesday demo and what he can defer.
thoughts: Truth: USD is an open Pixar standard, not NVIDIA-proprietary. For a Tuesday demo he can treat .usd as a scene-file format and use the UI. Minimum subset: stage, prim, reference, xform -- one line each. Skip: layer composition, value resolution, schema authoring. Do NOT link the 400-page spec. Do NOT promise he can 'ignore USD' -- error messages are USD-shaped.
code:
kb = lookup_knowledge(
    query="Isaac Sim 5.x USD minimum for beginner stage prim reference xform what to skip"
)
starters = scene_aware_starter_prompts(
    user_goal="understand just enough USD to run a demo Tuesday",
    user_level="beginner"
)
# Talking points to deliver:
# - USD = Pixar open standard, not NVIDIA-only
# - stage = the scene file you opened
# - prim = one node in the scene tree (robot, light, camera)
# - reference = 'include this other usd file here'
# - xform = position/rotation of a prim (~Unity Transform)
# Skip for now: layers, composition arcs, schema authoring, variants
failure_modes: Linking the OpenUSD spec PDF to a first-time user under deadline pressure
Claiming he can fully ignore USD (errors still speak in USD terms)
Recommending glTF as a 'simpler alternative' when Isaac Sim's native is USD
